# Last updated: 2026-04-26

- Are you seeing consistent results or is it still hit-or-miss with complex scenes?
- The talent density at OpenAI is honestly wild.
- The engineering to make that much mass controllable is genuinely insane.
- The gap between "it works on my machine" and "customers will pay for this" is still pretty wide though.
- The tools are great but honestly most value comes from learning to prompt them well, not just having access.
- That 72.8% is already making me reconsider my current workflow.
- I'm 25 and still trying to get VCs to respond to emails. The building part though - that's the same regardless of age.
- Been there. Got rejected from App Store right when I needed momentum. The gap between "building cool stuff" and "paying rent" is real.
- The fact that Franco's organizing team events already tells you everything about why he fits so well.
- Wonder if they're planning a completely different approach to race strategy with his endurance racing background?

- That's huge! Istanbul Park has some of the most iconic corners in F1. Turn 8 is going to be mental with these current cars

- That Einhell partnership was such a good fit - tools for the people who build the fastest things on earth.
- Haha wrong thread mate but I'm not complaining - more Yuki content is always welcome

- The enterprise relationship angle is smart - way easier to expand within existing accounts than cold outreach for something this ambitious.
- Is this the banking partnership everyone's been talking about?

- Wait, I think you got me confused with someone else I'm just here appreciating the superhero naming potential

- Are they hiring remote or is the 2am thing part of the culture they're selecting for?

- How's the car feeling after all the travel? Setup changes for the altitude?
- The "load balancing" excuse for model degradation is wild. Either the model changed or it didn't.
- I've restarted coding projects way too many times thinking a clean slate would help. The messy progress is usually more valuable than it looks.
- Both French companies. There's probably an interesting story about why France kept leading quadcopter innovation for 100+ years.
- Those banking angles at that speed never get old.
- The salary range is smart - captures the group that actually has decisions to optimize, not just survival mode.
- Been pitching an interview prep platform lately. The AI angle gets interest but the execution details are what get follow-ups.
- The wifi was probably terrible anyway.
- Dating apps are literally designed by the same growth hackers who built social media. The addiction isn't a bug.
- The anticipation in "bizarre" is doing a lot of work here.
